rulings.Case Title: THEODORE T. SHIFF TRUST DATED 1982 et al VS. GARY L. BRIDGE REVOCABLE TRUST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Adn Motion To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 23, 2010, line 2, PLAINTIFFS THEODORE SHIFF, GLORIA SHIFF, THEODORE T. SHIFF MD TRUST DATED 1982 Motion to File First Amended Complaint. Is granted. Plaintiffs' motion complies with California Rules of Court. Plaintiffs claims are not clearly barred as a matter of law and the issue may be raised in a summary adjudication motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR BOLANO VS. BURLINGTON COAT FACTORY, BUSINESS FORM UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 23, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT BURLINGTON COAT FACTORY OF CALIFORNIA, LLC, SALVADOR MARROQUIN AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off Calendar, in view of Court's 6/10/2010 ruling. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR BOLANO VS. BURLINGTON COAT FACTORY, BUSINESS FORM UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 23, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT BURLINGTON COAT FACTORY OF CALIFORNIA, LLC, SALVADOR MARROQUIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Mtn. for summ. judg.denied. Motion for summ. adj. granted as to 1st, 2nd, and 3rd causes of action. Plft agrees to ismiss 3rd cause of action. Deft, for purposes of this motion only, concedes that plft can make a prima facie case for discrimination. BCF puts forth credible, admissible evidence of a egitimate non-discriminatory reason for terminating plft: job-abandonment (extended absence from a work without approval.) The BCF Employee Handbook provides that an employee who fails to call or show up for work for three consecutively scheduled work days will be deemed to have abandoned their job. (Davis decl., Exh. A.) Regional H.R. partner Davis & District anager Close discussed & denied plft's request for time off based on the needs of the usiness & Vacation Blockout Period. (Davis's Decl.) Davis approved the termination on 3/15/2008for job abandonment when plft failed to call or show up for three days (Davis Decl. Parag 11) and plft's request for time off contains no managerial signature on the approval line (Davis Decl. Exh. C) contrary to the approval form for the Dec. 2006 vacation (Plft's Exh. B.) The burden shifts to plft to raise a triable issue of fact by offering substantial responsive evidence that he proffered reason was a pretext for age discrimination. Plft fails to meet this burden. The "hire little boys" comment is vague, & plft cannot recall when it was specifically said or tie it to his termination. Plft, age 49 at the time of his termination, testified that there were a lot of workers over age 40, including two in the late 60's. (Plft's depo 168:10-169:11.) Plft's replacement was a female who was going to turn age 40 within the year & thus was about to become a member of the protected class. Plft presents no evidence of discriminatory animus on the part of Davis and Close who decided to terminate

rulings.Case Title: PRIMO ANGELI VS. THEODORE ROOSEVELT ASSOCIATION, A NONPROFIT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint And / Or Stay Or Dismiss Action; Declarations Of Tweed Roosvelt And Howard Ehrlich In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 23, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT THEODORE ROOSEVELT ASSOCIATION Motion to Quash Service of Summons and Complaint and / or Stay or Dismiss Action. Is continued to 7/8/2010, opposing party to provide courtesy copy with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN JAMES BELISLE VS. DAVID A. SHERMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 23, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT DAVID SHERMAN DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 3rd cause of action for fraud and 4th cause of action for constructive fraud. The alleged misrepresentation on which they are based do not state a claim with respect to plaintiff. Plaintiff does not allege that a misrepresentation was made to him or that he relied on it. Demurrer overruled as to all other causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BRET LUEDER VS. BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 23, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead compliance with the Tort Claims presentation requirement and to state a statutory basis for liability against the Department of Motor Vehicles. =(302/CWW)
